definition_en: A prediction of what the weather will be like
example: According to the forecast, it will rain all weekend.

definition_en: Warm and damp weather with a lot of moisture in the air
example: The summer in Buenos Aires is very humid.

definition_en: A long period of little or no rainfall
example: The drought destroyed crops across the region.

definition_en: A severe snowstorm with strong winds
example: The blizzard left the city completely covered in snow.

definition_en: A period of abnormally hot weather lasting several days
example: The heatwave caused power cuts across the country.

definition_en: Covered with clouds and dull
example: It was an overcast day with no sunshine at all.

definition_en: Ice crystals that form on surfaces when the temperature drops below zero
example: There was frost on the windscreen this morning.

definition_en: A very strong wind
example: The gale knocked down several trees in the park.

definition_en: Any form of water that falls from the sky, including rain and snow
example: The monthly precipitation in this region is very low.

definition_en: A flash of bright light in the sky produced during a storm
example: Lightning struck the tree during the storm.

definition_en: The loud sound that follows lightning during a storm
example: We heard thunder in the distance.

definition_en: Unpleasantly warm and humid
example: It was so muggy that nobody wanted to go outside.

definition_en: Rain mixed with snow or ice
example: The sleet made driving conditions very dangerous.

definition_en: A light, gentle wind
example: A cool breeze made the hot afternoon bearable.

definition_en: Full of fog, making it hard to see
example: The foggy morning caused delays at the airport.

definition_en: Long-term shifts in global temperatures and weather patterns
example: Climate change is causing more extreme weather events.

definition_en: A violent rotating column of air extending from a storm cloud
example: The tornado destroyed dozens of homes in minutes.

definition_en: The weight of air pressing down on the Earth's surface
example: Low atmospheric pressure usually means rain is coming.

definition_en: Pleasantly warm and not extreme
example: The winters here are quite mild compared to the north.

definition_en: Extremely hot
example: It was a scorching day — over 40 degrees.

definition_en: A place giving protection from bad weather
example: We sheltered under a tree during the rain.

definition_en: One of the four periods of the year: spring, summer, autumn, winter
example: Autumn is my favourite season because of the colours.
